articles.description: George Russell takes an imposing pole position as Mercedes dominate a qualifying session for the Australian Grand Prix in which Max Verstappen crashed.
articles.title: Australian Grand Prix: George Russell takes Melbourne pole after Max Verstappen crash

articles.description: "It's a Grand Slam or disappointment" for France, writes Tom English as fellow risk takers Scotland bid to keep their own title hopes alive at Murrayfield.
articles.title: Six Nations 2026: Can Scotland prevent Grand Slam procession for France?

articles.description: Despite a torrid Premier League campaign, could winning the FA Cup keep Liverpool manager Arne Slot in a job?
articles.title: Wolves 1-3 Liverpool: Arne Slot's side reach FA Cup quarter-finals

articles.description: Former world 100m champion Fred Kerley is banned for two years for an anti-doping whereabouts violation.
articles.title: Fred Kerley: American former world 100m champion given two-year ban

articles.description: Daniel Berger extends his lead to five shots at the Arnold Palmer Invitational as Rory McIlroy rallies in the second round.
articles.title: Arnold Palmer Invitational: Daniel Berger leads by five as Rory McIlroy rallies in second round

articles.description: Para-alpine skier Varvara Voronchikhina wins Russia's first medal of the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics with downhill bronze.
articles.title: Winter Paralympics 2026: Varvara Voronchikhina wins Russia's first medal of Milan-Cortina Games

articles.description: Emma Raducanu makes an impressive start at Indian Wells as the British number one records a straight-set victory over Russian Anastasia Zakharova.
articles.title: Emma Raducanu: British number one impresses in Indian Wells opener

articles.description: Several names - including those of two former club heroes - are in the running to take the reins at Tottenham, while Serie A champions Napoli are looking to secure the future of Scott McTominay.
articles.title: Football gossip: Roberto de Zerbi, Glenn Hoddle, Robbie Keane, Scott McTominay, Phil Foden

articles.description: Explosions, fighter jets and aborted border crossings - British doubles player Henry Patten on finally getting out of Dubai amid the Middle East conflict.
articles.title: Henry Patten: Escaping Dubai amid Middle East conflict

articles.description: Like they did against Italy, Ireland dig deep at home to deny Wales a huge Six Nations shock and keep their title hopes alive.
articles.title: Ireland 27-17 Wales: Resilient Irish deny 'tremendous' visitors famous Six Nations win in Dublin
